entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A legume crop evaluated for reproductive efficiency and yield under high temperature conditions in the Sahel.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of the plant to flower and set pods, used as a measure of performance.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total agricultural output of the crop, assessed via growth rate and partitioning.

entities.name: HIGH TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental stress condition that affects the ability of cultivars to flower and set pods.

entities.name: SAHELIAN ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The ecological region where the study was conducted.

entities.name: ICRISAT SAHELIAN CENTER
entities.description: The specific research facility in Niger where the cultivars were evaluated.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted.

entities.name: CROP GROWTH RATE
entities.description: A measure of biomass accumulation used to assess differences in yield among cultivars.

entities.name: PARTITIONING
entities.description: The distribution of biomass among plant organs used to assess yield differences.

entities.name: POD SET
entities.description: The percentage of flowers that successfully develop into pods.

entities.name: TN88-63
entities.description: A widely adapted cowpea cultivar that averaged 59% pod set.

entities.name: A73-2-1
entities.description: A cowpea cultivar that averaged 56% pod set.

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE PERIOD
entities.description: The duration of the phase during which the plant flowers and sets pods.
